title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen investigates the death of Andrew Van, a reclusive school principal in Arroyo who was known as a teetotaler and a model teacher. Despite local rumors of his ungodliness spread by Weldon newspapers, Ellery finds little information about Van's past. During an autopsy at the county courthouse, Coroner Stapleton reveals that Van's body has been stored for eight days and is headless. Ellery observes the victim's belongings, including a wallet, a watch, and a letter from a Pittsburgh bookstore, noting that some items bear the initials "AV."

During the proceedings, farmer Michael O'Keefe testifies that he and "Old Pete" discovered Van's body nailed by the hands and feet to a signpost near Arroyo Peak. Old Pete, a disheveled man claiming to have been dead for twenty years, is clarified by Matt Hollis to be a local eccentric who lives in a small house near Arroyo. Mayor Hollis and Sheriff Rudd testify regarding the discovery of the body and a T-shaped mark found on a door at the Fanns' residence.

Luther Bernheim, the local grocer, testifies that while Van was a regular customer who paid in cash, he was never truly friendly. Bernheim notes that Van had unusual tastes, specifically ordering expensive caviar. This detail leads Ellery to suspect that Van's past was more luxurious than his current life suggested. Finally, District Attorney Crummett testifies that Van arrived in Arroyo nine years prior to take the teaching position, living on Arroyo Street with a servant named Klein.
